text:
A Michael Kors Bag, Suede Slippers and More Holiday Gifts at Walmart — Starting at $9



Drew Weisholtz

Athletes aremaking historyat the2024 Summer Olympics, while the TODAY crew is basking in history.

TODAY’s Savannah Guthrie, Hoda Kotb and Al Roker got to visit the Palace of Versailles and soak in its beauty and majesty.

Located 10 miles outside of Paris, the Palace of Versailles was a hunting lodge that was transformed into a home for French royalty by Louis XIV in the 17th century. Kings and queens lived at Versailles for more than a century until the French Revolution did away with monarchy in France.

“Kings walked here right where we’re walking. Queens walked here right where we’re walking,” Hoda said in a segment that aired July 31, while she, Savannah and Al walked on the grounds outside.

“And now two more queens are walking — and a court jester,” Al quipped about the trio.

The rooms are stunning, flush with gold and mirrors and paintings on the ceiling. Marie Antoinette lived there, and her apartment blew away Savannah, Hoda and Al.

“Oh, my!” Savannah and Hoda said in unison when seeing it.

“That is insane,” Hoda said while marveling at the canopy over the bed.

“That’s crazy,” Al said.

A hall of mirrors accents the affluence of Versailles. It’s a long corridor filled with mirrors, which were a luxury hundreds of years ago.

“This is incredible,” Savannah said.

“Stunning. Stunning,” echoed Hoda.

Versailles director Laurent Salomé said the hall was not just decorative.

“It was used mainly for diplomatic official events, sometimes for balls. Everybody’s come here. All the artists, musicians, Mozart, everyone,” he said.

Salomé also said Versailles, which currently hosts the largest-ever art exhibition about horses, in honor of the Olympic equestrian venue there, is as large as it is opulent.

“Altogether it’s 2,300 rooms. I’ve been here seven years, and I’m not sure I’ve seen all the rooms,” he said.

Savannah, Hoda and Al were also given a key to open a door to a chapel, which Salomé helped them access.

“It’s beautiful,” Savannah said when they walked in, while Al noticed the light beaming through.

“It’s one of the most beautiful chapels in France, and probably in the world,” Salomé said.

Salomé then gave the TODAY trio another thrill when he let them open a window, “which is something you normally do not do,” he said. “No one’s allowed to do this.”

He let the TODAY crew open the door, giving them the opportunity to do something one very big name was not allowed to do during a recent visit.

“Even the king of England recently,Charles III, we thought it was not appropriate to open that door. But I don’t know. Today is a special day. The weather is nice. You have the Olympics,” he said.

Salomé pulled open the giant window and invited them to step out onto a balcony.

“This is so crazy,” Al said.

“This is so cool,” Hoda said.

“Laurent, you have given us a moment we will never forget our whole lives,” Savannah said.

Drew Weisholtz is a reporter for TODAY Digital, focusing on pop culture, nostalgia and trending stories. He has seen every episode of “Saved by the Bell” at least 50 times, longs to perfect the crane kick from “The Karate Kid” and performs stand-up comedy, while also cheering on the New York Yankees and New York Giants. A graduate of Rutgers University, he is the married father of two kids who believe he is ridiculous.

title: Savannah Guthrie, Hoda Kotb, Al Roker Tour Palace Of Versailles On TODAY Show
description: The TODAY show's Savannah Guthrie, Hoda Kotb and Al Roker tour the Palace of Versailles during the 2024 Summer Olympics in Paris, France.
